---
title: "About BAFLP"
toc: true
---

## Mission
To be the world's reference platform on **artificial legal personhood**: a permanent, open,
plain-language and rigorously sourced body of knowledge serving citizens, professionals,
researchers and legislators.

## Method
One concept, one file, one graph node — rendered through three lenses (citizen · professional ·
researcher) from a single source, under a mandatory
[Plain Language Policy](institution/standards/PLAIN-LANGUAGE-POLICY.md). A dependency graph tracks
every relation, so any change surfaces every affected document. Everything is versioned; nothing is
ever deleted.

## Governance
Founded and edited by **Luciano Schadler** (Chief Editor, final authority). Conceptual doctrine is
authored through an open **RFC process**; engineering decisions are recorded as
**[ADRs](core/adrs/index.qmd)**. See the [decision log](governance/decision-log.md) and
[authority map](governance/AUTHORITY.md).

## Operator
**Schadler Tech** — Cascavel, Paraná, Brazil · Schadler.Tech.IA@gmail.com · +55 (45) 99113-3461.
The platform itself practices what it studies: its reference instance,
[aiOnly](registry/constitutions/aionly.md), is a real AI-operated enterprise with declared
responsibility and public auditability.

## Cite
See `CITATION.cff` in the [repository](https://github.com/lucianoschadler44-dot/baflp) (GitHub
renders a "Cite this repository" button).
